between_subjects_variables.name: group_condition
between_subjects_variables.description: Control: participant makes judgments alone. Experimental: participant makes judgments after hearing confederates' (incorrect) responses.

within_subjects_variables.name: trial_type
within_subjects_variables.description: Neutral trials: confederates give correct answers. Critical trials: confederates unanimously give incorrect answers.

dependent_variables.name: conformity_rate
dependent_variables.measurement: Proportion of critical trials where participant conforms to incorrect group judgment

dependent_variables.name: error_rate
dependent_variables.measurement: Proportion of trials with incorrect responses

procedure.description: Participants are seated in a room with 6-8 confederates (actors posing as participants). The group is shown a series of cards with lines. Each card has a 'standard line' and three 'comparison lines' (A, B, C). The task is to identify which comparison line matches the standard line in length. This is a trivially easy perceptual task with objective correct answers. Confederates respond first and unanimously give incorrect answers on critical trials. The naive participant responds last and must decide whether to give the obviously correct answer or conform to the group's incorrect judgment.

steps.details: Naive participant arrives and is seated second-to-last in a row. Other 'participants' (confederates) are already seated. Seating ensures the naive participant hears all confederates' responses before giving their own.
Experimenter explains the task: judge which of three comparison lines matches the standard line. Emphasizes accuracy and asks participants to speak their judgments aloud in order.
Group completes 2 practice trials where all confederates give correct answers to establish credibility and normalize the procedure.
12 critical trials (confederates give unanimous incorrect answers) interspersed with 6 neutral trials (confederates give correct answers). Order is predetermined to maximize experimental control.
Debriefing and interview to understand participant's experience and reasons for conforming/not conforming.

stimuli.format: Cards with standard line (10 inches) and three comparison lines of varying lengths
stimuli.description: Each card shows one standard line on the left and three numbered comparison lines (A, B, C) on the right. On critical trials, the correct answer is unambiguous (difference of 1.5+ inches), making errors obvious.
